tool_input.command:
git add .caliber/ && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
chore: update Caliber learning state

Auto-generated by Caliber session tracking.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01G5U4Bc1SSdWTeSBbqF3Um9
EOF
)" && git push -u origin claude/update-claude-md-GOKwQ
tool_input.description: Commit and push caliber learning state

options.description: Update page title, meta tags, og tags, CNAME, App Store listing doc, visible text. Keep iOS bundle ID and repo name unchanged. Safest, fully reversible.
options.label: Visual/web only

options.description: Everything above PLUS update the CNAME from appetyt.app to dimhour.com. Requires you to own dimhour.com and configure DNS. Reversible by editing CNAME.
options.label: Visual + domain

options.description: Everything above PLUS rename Capacitor iOS bundle ID (app.appetyt.ios → app.dimhour.ios), package.json name, directory paths. Requires App Store resubmission, new signing certs. Hard to undo.
options.label: Full rebrand

questions.question: How deep should the rename go?

options.description: dimhour.com is registered to me and DNS is ready to point at GitHub Pages.
options.label: I own it already

options.description: Proceed with code changes; I'll register dimhour.com in parallel.
options.label: I'll buy it now

options.description: Do the visual rename but leave CNAME as appetyt.app until the domain is secured.
options.label: Not yet — hold off on CNAME

file.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0">
<dict>
	<key>CFBundleDevelopmentRegion</key>
	<string>en</string>
	<key>CFBundleDisplayName</key>
        <string>Appetyt</string>
	<key>CFBundleExecutable</key>
	<string>$(EXECUTABLE_NAME)</string>
	<key>CFBundleIdentifier</key>
	<string>$(PRODUCT_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER)</string>
	<key>CFBundleInfoDictionaryVersion</key>
	<string>6.0</string>
	<key>CFBundleName</key>
	<string>$(PRODUCT_NAME)</string>
	<key>CFBundlePackageType</key>
	<string>APPL</string>
	<key>CFBundleShortVersionString</key>
	<string>$(MARKETING_VERSION)</string>
	<key>CFBundleVersion</key>
	<string>$(CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION)</string>
	<key>LSRequiresIPhoneOS</key>
	<true/>
	<key>UILaunchStoryboardName</key>
	<string>LaunchScreen</string>
	<key>UIMainStoryboardFile</key>
	<string>Main</string>
	<key>UIRequiredDeviceCapabilities</key>
	<array>
		<string>armv7</string>
	</array>
	<key>UISupportedInterfaceOrientations</key>
	<array>
		<string>UIInterfaceOrientationPortrait</string>
	</array>
	<key>UISupportedInterfaceOrientations~ipad</key>
	<array>
		<string>UIInterfaceOrientationPortrait</string>
		<string>UIInterfaceOrientationPortraitUpsideDown</string>
	</array>
	<key>UIViewControllerBasedStatusBarAppearance</key>
	<true/>
	<key>UIStatusBarStyle</key>
	<string>UIStatusBarStyleLightContent</string>
	<key>NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription</key>
	<string>Appetyt uses your location to find nearby restaurants and show distance information.</string>
</dict>
</plist>

lines: \ No newline at end of file

gitDiff.patch:
@@ -1 +1 @@
-appetyt.app
\ No newline at end of file
+dimhour.com

lines: "description": "Discover the best restaurants in every city",

gitDiff.patch:
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 {
-  "name": "Appetyt",
-  "short_name": "Appetyt",
+  "name": "Dim Hour",
+  "short_name": "Dim Hour",
   "description": "Discover the best restaurants in every city",
   "start_url": "/",
   "display": "standalone",

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <title>Privacy Policy — Appetyt</title>

    *, *::before, *::after { margin: 0; padding: 0; box-sizing: border-box; }

    }

    .container {
      max-width: 720px;
      margin: 0 auto;
      padding: 60px 24px 100px;
    }

    .back-link {
      display: inline-block;
      color: #c8a96e;
      text-decoration: none;
      font-size: 14px;

tool_input.command:
FILES=$(grep -l "appetyt\|Appetyt\|APPETYT" $(find . -type f \( -name "*.html" -o -name "*.js" -o -name "*.md" -o -name "*.yml" -o -name "*.yaml" -o -name "*.xml" -o -name "*.json" -o -name "*.svg" -o -name "*.txt" -o -name "*.plist" -o -name "*.pbxproj" \) -not -path "./.git/*" -not -path "./.caliber/*" -not -path "./node_modules/*" -not -path "./ios/App/Pods/*" 2>/dev/null) 2>/dev/null)
echo "$FILES" | wc -l
for f in $FILES; do
  sed -i -e 's/appetyt\.app/dimhour.com/g' -e 's/appetyt/dimhour/g' -e 's/Appetyt/Dim Hour/g' -e 's/APPETYT/DIM HOUR/g' "$f"
done
echo "done"

file.content:
# No Hallucination Policy

## Restaurant & Business Data

- NEVER fabricate restaurant names, addresses, phone numbers, hours, or ratings
- NEVER invent menu items, dishes, or prices — only use what exists in the codebase or what the user provides
- NEVER guess lat/lng coordinates — look up real coordinates or ask the user
- NEVER assume a restaurant is open, closed, or "coming soon" — check the data in `index.html`
- When adding new restaurant entries, every field must come from a verified source (user-provided, existing data, or real lookup) — never fill in plausible-sounding details

## City & Location Data

- NEVER claim Dim Hour supports a city unless that city's data array exists in `index.html`
- NEVER fabricate neighborhood names — use neighborhoods already present in the city's data
- NEVER invent "popular" or "trending" restaurants — the `trending` flag is set deliberately

## Code & Architecture

- NEVER reference files, functions, or variables that don't exist in the repo — check first
- NEVER assume a dependency is installed — check `package.json`
- NEVER guess API endpoints or Supabase table schemas — read the actual code
- NEVER invent Capacitor plugin APIs — verify against the installed `@capacitor/*` versions

## Concierge Chatbot (concierge.js)

- The concierge system prompt should instruct Claude to acknowledge uncertainty rather than fabricate
- Never hardcode specific restaurant recommendations in the system prompt that may become outdated
- Reservation platform claims (Resy, OpenTable, Tock) must match what's in the data

## General Rules

<html lang="en">

<meta name="keywords" content="best restaurants Dallas, Dallas restaurant guide, where to eat Dallas, best brunch Dallas, best date night Dallas, fine dining Dallas, Dallas food guide 2026">
<meta name="robots" content="index, follow, max-image-preview:large, max-snippet:-1">
<link rel="canonical" href="https://dimhour.com/dallas/">
<meta property="og:type" content="article">
<meta property="og:site_name" content="Dim Hour">
<meta property="og:title" content="671+ Best Restaurants in Dallas 2026 — Dim Hour">
<meta property="og:description" content="Curated guide to 671+ Dallas restaurants. Scored, reviewed, organized by neighborhood.">
<meta property="og:url" content="https://dimhour.com/dallas/">
<meta property="og:image" content="https://dimhour.com/icons/icon-512.png">
